question: Is lupus really that difficult to diagnose, and are new treatments actually effective?
topic: Corrects outdated perceptions about lupus diagnosis and treatment that prevent people from seeking a

sources.name: Handout on Health: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus — NIH/NIAMS
Cochrane Reviews on Systemic Lupus Erythematosus — Cochrane Library

discovery_sources.text: Lupus diagnosis currently takes months to years; previously took 2-3 years to diagnose (improved from past decade)
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: When The Diagnosis Is Thrown For A Lupus

discovery_sources.text: No FDA-approved lupus treatments existed for 50 years until first approval ~20 years ago
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: When The Diagnosis Is Thrown For A Lupus

discovery_sources.text: Three additional FDA-approved lupus interventions approved in recent years, with possibly two more in current year (2026)
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: When The Diagnosis Is Thrown For A Lupus

discovery_sources.text: CAR-T cell therapy first lupus patient treated in Germany in 2021, still in remission without treatment as of 2026
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: When The Diagnosis Is Thrown For A Lupus

discovery_sources.text: First CAR-T cell therapy lupus patient in New England treated in 2023, still lupus-free and treatment-free after ~3 years
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: When The Diagnosis Is Thrown For A Lupus

discovery_sources.text: Approximately 150 ongoing clinical trials for lupus and lupus nephritis worldwide
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: When The Diagnosis Is Thrown For A Lupus

discovery_sources.text: 40+ clinical trials currently underway testing cell therapy modalities for lupus
